PostToolUse.reason: Note: Full validation requires running cmake (heavy operation)

PostToolUse.description: Check Qt UI patterns & const correctness
PostToolUse.command: grep -n 'new Q' $FILE_PATH | head -5 || echo 'No Qt allocs detected'
PostToolUse.reason: Alert on potential Q_OBJECT hierarchy issues

PreToolUse.description: Warn if file will exceed 800 lines (ECC Standard)
PreToolUse.command: node -e "let d=''; process.stdin.on('data', c => d += c); process.stdin.on('end', () => { const lines = d.split('\\n').length; if (lines > 800) { console.error('[WARN] File will be ' + lines + ' lines (target: <800)'); process.exit(1); } else { console.log(d); }})"

Stop.description: Verify project builds cleanly at session end
Run unit tests at session end

metadata.description: Automation hooks for C++ + Qt project standards enforcement
metadata.ecc-integration: Hooks are invoked by ECC pre/post tool-use and session-end events
metadata.manual-trigger: User can manually run: `hookify run hook-id` to test individual hooks

items: cmake-validate: Requires cmake binary + project configuration
final-test: Requires CTest configuration in CMakeLists.txt
Install clang-format: `apt install clang-format` (Linux) or `brew install clang-format` (macOS)
Install cppcheck: `apt install cppcheck` or `brew install cppcheck`
Set `enabled: true` for disabled hooks after installing dependencies
Add new hook in PostToolUse array with unique id
Set matcher: Write (new files) or Edit (modifications)
Use filePattern glob: `src/**/*.cpp` matches src/ recursively
